summary: Stunning audience recording of the Wall‑of‑Sound debut with blazing Jerry Garcia guitar and a legendary Playin’ > U.J.B. > Dew sandwich.
review: The show is hailed as one of the band’s all‑time greats – a historic first full Wall of Sound, unstoppable rhythm section, and a second‑set jam that includes the famed Playin’ > UJB > Dew sequence.  The Scott Clugston audience transfer captures the mix with remarkable clarity, though a few vocal mic quirks and occasional transition roughness are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 25 enthusiastic reviews (average ~5 ★) praise both performance and sound; only minor technical complaints appear, yielding an almost perfect combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear capture of the Wall of Sound debut, superb instrument separation (Bill’s kick drum highlighted), occasional vocal mic dropout and a slightly high Bob vocal mix, but overall breathtaking fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but a bit uneven for some listeners; after early sound‑level tweaks the Wall of Sound settles and the second set erupts into a historic Playin’ > UJB > Dew sandwich, delivering a seamless, high‑energy flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar ‘blooms like fireworks’; tone described as perfect and he “played his ass off.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is called unstoppable; highlighted as a standout element of the recording.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals occasionally sit high in the mix and one reviewer notes a mic dropout early in the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s kick drum is praised for being superbly miked, especially on the China / Rider segment.
